unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities

questions.question: A gamer has $15 to spend on in-game items. Each item costs $2 and a special power-up costs $5. If the gamer wants to buy at least 2 items and at most 1 power-up, which inequality represents this situation?

A data analyst has 480 GB of storage space. Each data file requires 10 GB and each video file requires 50 GB. If the analyst wants to store at least 5 data files and at most 8 video files, which inequality represents this situation?

A coding team has 12 hours to complete a project. Each team member can work for at most 4 hours and must work for at least 2 hours. If there are 3 team members, which inequality represents this situation?

A gamer wants to level up in a game. To do so, they need to score at least 500 points in a level and have a accuracy of at least 80%. If the gamer scores $x$ points and has an accuracy of $y$%, which inequality represents this situation?
A data center has 1000 servers. Each server can handle at most 100 users and must handle at least 20 users. If there are $x$ servers and $y$ users, which inequality represents this situation?

A software company wants to release a new game. The game requires at least 500 MB of free space and a processing speed of at least 2.5 GHz. If a computer has $x$ MB of free space and a processing speed of $y$ GHz, which inequality represents this situation?

A tech firm has 15 employees. Each employee can work for at most 8 hours and must work for at least 4 hours. If the firm wants to complete a project that requires at least 80 hours of work, which inequality represents this situation?
A coding logic problem requires at least 3 lines of code and at most 10 lines of code. If a programmer writes $x$ lines of code, which inequality represents this situation?

A data analyst has 720 GB of storage space. Each data file requires 15 GB and each video file requires 60 GB. If the analyst wants to store at least 6 data files and at most 9 video files, write an inequality that represents this situation and solve for the feasible region.

A gamer wants to level up in a game. To do so, they need to score at least 600 points in a level and have an accuracy of at least 85%. If the gamer scores $x$ points and has an accuracy of $y$%, write an inequality that represents this situation and graph the feasible region.

teacher_rules: Read each question carefully and choose the correct answer.
Use the given information to determine the correct inequality.
Graph the feasible region to visualize the solution.
